title: ARPLookup - Batu33TR's Project
snippet: Ever wanted to scan IP addresses on the network? With ARPLookup script, it’s possible to detect devices connected to network with their MAC addresses.A Netdiscover similar script collects information by broadcasting ARP on the network and displays it in the form of a list. Written with Python 3. It is completely open source and supporting Linux, Windows and macOS (not tested) platforms. Requires administrator/sudo privileges to work. Source code are available hereInstalling on Linux Using this script on Linux is so easy. Install Python and Git with package managersudo apt install git python3 python3-pip # For Debian / Ubuntusudo pacman -S git python3 python3-pip # For Arch Linux / Manjarosudo zypper in git python3 python3-pip # For OpenSUSE Clone repository after install required packagesgit clone https://github.com/Batu33TR/ARPLookup Switch cloned directory and install dependencies for Pythonsudo pip install -r requirements.txt And you’re ready. Check installation with this command:kali@kali~$ python3 ARPLookup.pyARPLookup startedUsage: python3 arplookup.py -r &lt;IP_RANGE&gt;What about Windows?As you can see in the picture, ARPLookup.py says There’s no libpcap provider available!. But don’t worry, solution is easy.Installing NPCAP to Windows (Quotation) What is NPCAP: Npcap is the Nmap Project’s packet capture (and sending) library for Microsoft Windows. It implements the open Pcap API using a custom Windows kernel driver alongside our Windows build of the excellent libpcap library. This allows Windows software to capture raw network traffic (including wireless networks, wired ethernet, localhost traffic, and many VPNs) using a simple, portable API. Npcap allows for sending raw packets as well. Mac and Linux systems already include the Pcap API, so Npcap allows popular software such as Nmap and Wireshark to run on all these platforms (and more) with a single codebase. Go to https://npcap.com/#download Click Npcap Installer and download it. Follow the installation steps and npcap is ready.Python for Windows Now install Python for Windows and don’t for get to tick “Add Python to PATH”Download source Download ARPLookup source as ZIP and extract to Desktop Install required packages for Pythonpip install -r requirements.txtAnd run it Open a CMD/PowerShell as Administrator and switch extracted folder. Example:PS C:\Users\Batu33TR&gt; cd .\Desktop\ARPLookupPS C:\Users\Batu33TR\Desktop\ARPLookup&gt; Now test ARPLookup if script works correctlyPS C:\Users\Batu33TR\Desktop\ARPLookup&gt; python .\ARPLookup.pyARPLookup startedUsage: python arplookup.py -r &lt;IP_RANGE&gt;PS C:\Users\Batu33TR\Desktop\ARPLookup&gt; And kaboom, ARPLookup is ready to use on Windows. Congratulations.UsageDetecting subnet mask range First, open a terminal and type the following command:kali@kali~$ ifconfig # For LinuxPS C:\Users\Batu33TR&gt; ipconfig # For Windows As seen in the terminal output in the picture, the network interface name is eth0 and it uses subnet 255.255.255.0 mask as 192.168.28.0 so this subnet is 24 bit.Time to run script and detect devices Now, time to scan connected devices and detect IP with MAC. Switch to ARPLookup directory and run the scriptkali@kali~$ sudo python3 ARPLookup.py -r 192.168.28.0/24 After ~5 seconds, devices will be detect and listed by ARPLookup. Congratulations, ARPLookup run was successfully completed. As can be seen in the picture, devices connected to the network have been detected.Warning The devices shown in the pictures are all is just virtual machines and connected to private virtual network. No 3rd party device, network or person is targeted here. Use it only on networks you are authorized. This script is for testing purposes only!
